name: Chirashi Zushi
recipeIngredient: 2 dried shitaki mushrooms, trimmed and sliced thinly
1/4 oz. seasoned, canned bamboo shoots, diced
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak the shitaki mushrooms in lukewarm water until soft (30 minutes). Reserve the liquid and trim off the stems, slicing the mushrooms thinly.
2. Peel and cut the carrot into 2-inch matchsticks.
3. Combine dashi stock, shitaki soaking water, mirin, soy sauce, and sake in a pot. Cook the carrots in this mixture until tender. Add the shitaki mushrooms and cook until done.
4. Dice the bamboo shoots into half-inch cubes.
5. Mix the cooked carrot, shitaki mushrooms, bamboo shoots, radishes, bok choy, and sushi rice together.
6. Slice the snow peas for garnish.
7. Decorate with raisins, sliced snow peas, and hard-boiled egg whites.
Dashi Stock:
1 oz. dried kombu (10 inches)
1 cup soybean sprouts
1. Rinse the kombu clean without removing its coating of white dust. Place in a large saucepan with 6 cups cold water. Bring to a simmer over low heat and cook for 5 minutes. Remove the kombu and discard it.
2. Add the soybean sprouts and continue cooking on low heat for an additional 5 minutes. Once boiling, remove from heat and strain through a cheesecloth-lined sieve, discarding the sprouts. The stock can be stored refrigerated for up to 3 days or frozen for up to 6 months.
